back_translation: After Moses, the servant of the Lord, had passed away, the Lord spoke to Joshua son of Nun, the one who attended on Moses, saying:
"Moses my servant is dead. Now arise and cross over this Jordan River, you and all this people, into the land that I am about to give to them, namely the descendants of Israel.
Every place where the soles of your-pl feet will tread, I have given to you-pl, just as I spoke to Moses.
From the wilderness and this Lebanon as far as the great river, the Euphrates River, all the land of the Hittites, and as far as the Great Sea to the west, shall be your-pl boundary.
No one will be able to stand firm against you-sg all the days of your-sg life. As I was with Moses, so I will be with you-sg; I will not abandon you-sg and I will not forsake you-sg at all.
Be strong and courageous, for you-sg will lead these people into possession of the land that I swore to their forefathers that I would give as an inheritance.

Above all, be strong and exceedingly courageous; be careful to do according to all the law that Moses my servant commanded you-sg. Do not turn aside from that law either to the right or to the left, so that you-sg may have success wherever you-sg go.

Do not let this book of the law depart from your-sg mouth; meditate on it day and night, so that you-sg may be careful to do according to everything written in it; for then you-sg will prosper in your-sg way, and then you-sg will have success.
Have I not commanded you-sg? Be strong and courageous; do not be terrified and do not be discouraged, for the Lord your-sg God is with you-sg wherever you-sg go."
Then Joshua commanded the officers of the people, saying:

"Go throughout the camp and command the people, saying: 'Prepare provisions for yourselves, for in three more days you-pl will cross over this Jordan River, to enter and take possession of the land that the Lord your-pl God is about to give you-pl as a possession.'"
And Joshua said to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh:
"Remember the word that Moses the servant of the Lord commanded you-pl, saying: 'The Lord your-pl God will grant you-pl rest, and will give you-pl this land.'

Your-pl wives, little children, and herds-of-livestock will remain in the land that Moses gave you-pl on the east side of the Jordan River. But all the valiant warriors of you-pl must take arms and cross over ahead of your-pl brothers, and you-pl shall help them,

until the Lord grants rest to your-pl brothers just as you-pl received, and they themselves take possession of the land that the Lord your-pl God is about to give them. Then you-pl may return to the land of your-pl inheritance and take possession of the land that Moses the servant of the Lord gave you-pl on the east side of the Jordan River."
They answered Joshua, "All that you have commanded us, we will do; and wherever you send us, we will go.
Just as we obeyed Moses in all things, so we will obey you. Only may the Lord your God be with you, just as He was with Moses.
Whoever rebels against your command and does not obey your words in everything you order, that one shall be put to death. Above all, may you be strong and courageous!"
